channel.title: EDGAR Live Scanner
channel.description: High-signal SEC EDGAR filings — tender offers, going-private, activist stakes, spinoffs, delistings, busted deals.

item.title: 🪙 Debt Restr. · CRMT
item.ns0:headline: Lender forbearance through June 12; strategic review with Houlihan Lokey
item.description:
Lender forbearance through June 12; strategic review with Houlihan Lokey

America's Car-Mart received forbearance from Silver Point Finance-led lenders through June 12, 2026, after failing minimum liquidity and collateral coverage covenants. The Special Committee, advised by Houlihan Lokey and FTI Consulting, is evaluating financing, recapitalization, restructuring, and M&A options. A retention program for executives includes cash awards and stock options tied to continued employment and change-in-control.

About: America's Car-Mart, Inc., through its subsidiaries, operates as an automotive retailer for the used car market in the United States. It sells older model used vehicles and provides financing for its customers. The company was founded in 1981 and is headquartered in Rogers, Arkansas.

  • Counterparty: Silver Point Finance (agent for lenders)
  • Announced: 2026-06-01

Px $7.64 · Mkt cap $65.2M · EV $900.0M · Fwd EV/EBITDA 14.3x · Consumer Discretionary / Specialty Retail

item.title: 🤝 Merger · CEPT
item.ns0:headline: Acquires Securitize; S-4 effective, special meeting June 29
item.description:
Acquires Securitize; S-4 effective, special meeting June 29

Cantor Equity Partners II (CEPT) to acquire Securitize, Inc. via business combination; the combined company will be named Securitize Corp. and list on NYSE under ticker SECZ. The SEC declared the S-4 effective; CEPT shareholders of record as of May 11, 2026 will vote on the merger at a special meeting scheduled for June 29, 2026. Closing is expected shortly after approval, subject to customary conditions.

About: Cantor Equity Partners II, Inc., does not have significant operations. It intends to focus on effecting a merger, share exchange, asset acquisition, share purchase, reorganization or similar business combination with one or more businesses. The company was formerly known as CF International Acquisition Corp. III. The company was incorporated in 2020 and is based in New York, New York.

  • Announced: 2025-10-27
  • Expected Close: Q3 2026

Px $12.14 · Mkt cap $385.9M · EV $386.5M · Financials / Capital Markets

item.ns0:headline: Merging with Cantor Equity Partners SPAC; S-4 effective; vote June 29
item.description:
Merging with Cantor Equity Partners SPAC; S-4 effective; vote June 29

Moves closer to becoming a publicly traded company via its previously announced business combination with Cantor Equity Partners II (CEPT). The SEC declared effective the S-4 registration statement. CEPT's special meeting to approve the merge is set for June 29, 2026; closing expected shortly thereafter. The combined company will trade as SECZ on the NYSE.

item.title: 🤝 Merger · RAAQ
item.ns0:headline: Acquires IQM Finland in $2.3B stock + $146M PIPE; vote June 25
item.description:
Acquires IQM Finland in $2.3B stock + $146M PIPE; vote June 25

A SPAC, is merging with IQM Finland Oy, a quantum computing company. Each RAAQ Class A ordinary share will be exchanged for one IQM American Depositary Share (ADS). A concurrent PIPE of approximately $146 million at $10 per ADS supports the transaction. The combined entity will list on Nasdaq under the symbol 'IQMX' (warrants: IQMX WS). The extraordinary general meeting to approve the business combination is scheduled for June 25, 2026.

About: Real Asset Acquisition Corp. does not have significant operations. It focuses on effecting a merger, amalgamation, share exchange, asset acquisition, share purchase, reorganization, or similar business combination with one or more businesses in the metals and mining, real estate, infrastructure, and related sectors. The company was incorporated in 2024 and is based in Princeton, New Jersey.

  • Counterparty: IQM Finland Oy
  • Offer price: 1 IQM ADS per RAAQ share
  • Deal value: ~$2.3B
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-02-22

Px $11.11 · Mkt cap $263.8M · EV $263.0M · Financials / Capital Markets

item.title: 🤝 Merger · BN
item.ns0:headline: Simplifies structure via arrangement with Brookfield Wealth Solutions; vote July 16
item.description:
Simplifies structure via arrangement with Brookfield Wealth Solutions; vote July 16

Brookfield Corporation seeks shareholder approval for a plan of arrangement to simplify its corporate structure by combining with Brookfield Wealth Solutions (BWS) under one publicly traded company, Brookfield Corporation Ltd. The transaction requires approval from shareholders of both BN and BWS at their respective meetings; BN's meeting is scheduled for July 16, 2026. The arrangement is intended to consolidate ownership under a single public entity.

About: Brookfield Corporation is a multi-asset manager focused on real estate, credit, renewable power and transition, infrastructure, venture capital, and private equity including growth capital and emerging growth investments. It manages a range of public and private investment products and services for institutional and retail clients. It typically makes investments in sizeable, premier assets across geographies and asset classes.

  • Counterparty: Brookfield Wealth Solutions Ltd.
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-05-26

Px $44.60 · Mkt cap $100.63B · EV $477.48B · Financials / Capital Markets

item.description:
Acquired by Brookfield Corp. via plan of arrangement; vote July 16

BNT (Brookfield Wealth Solutions) enters into a plan of arrangement with Brookfield Corporation (BN) to combine under a single publicly traded entity, Brookfield Corporation Ltd. Shareholders of BNT will vote on the transaction at a special meeting on July 16, 2026. A Return of Capital Resolution is also up for vote.

About: Brookfield Wealth Solutions Ltd., through its subsidiaries, provides retirement services, wealth protection products, and capital solutions to individuals and institutions. It operates through Annuities, Property and Casualty (P&C), Life Insurance segments. The Annuities segment offers retail and institutional annuities, including fixed index, fixed rate, single premium immediate, and variable annuities, as well as pension risk transfer.

item.ns0:headline: Terminates M2i Global merger; exploring strategic alternatives
item.description:
Terminates M2i Global merger; exploring strategic alternatives

Terminated the M2i Global merger agreement because the merger was not consummated by the March 31, 2026 outside date. No termination fee incurred. The company has received unsolicited letters of interest and is evaluating strategic alternatives, though no transaction is guaranteed.

About: Volato Group, Inc. operates as a private aviation company in the United States. The company offers fractional ownership, aircraft management, jet cards, deposit, and charter programs. It also sells airplanes; and provides Vaunt, a software-as-a-service subscription platform. In addition, the company offers Mission Control, a cloud-based software which provides a robust, API-first solution streamlining critical functions across flight scheduling, customer relationship management (CRM), and crew m…

item.title: 📤 Divestiture · FSUN
item.ns0:headline: Sells $890M multifamily loan pool to Brookfield; proceeds for deposit paydown
item.description:
Sells $890M multifamily loan pool to Brookfield; proceeds for deposit paydown

FirstSun Capital Bancorp's subsidiary Sunflower Bank closed the sale of approximately $890 million of performing multifamily commercial real estate loans to entities affiliated with Brookfield Asset Management. The sale was part of a balance sheet repositioning strategy following the acquisition of First Foundation Inc., which closed on April 1, 2026. Proceeds will be used to pay down high-cost brokered and non-brokered deposits acquired from First Foundation. FirstSun expects to complete the remaining loan downsizing before the end of the second quarter of 2026.

About: FirstSun Capital Bancorp operates as the bank holding company for Sunflower Bank, National Association that provides commercial and consumer banking and financial services to small and medium-sized companies in the United States. It operates through Banking and Mortgage Operations segments. The company offers noninterest and interest-bearing deposit accounts, checking and savings accounts, money market and term certificate accounts, and treasury management products and services, as well as certi…

  • Counterparty: Brookfield Asset Management
  • Deal value: $890 million
  • Consideration: cash
  • Expected Close: 2026-06-04

item.title: 🤝 Merger · CEPO
item.ns0:headline: De-SPAC with BSTR (Bitcoin fund) at $10/SPAC share; vote June 26
item.description:
De-SPAC with BSTR (Bitcoin fund) at $10/SPAC share; vote June 26

Is merging with BSTR Holdings, a Bitcoin-backed entity, via a de-SPAC transaction. Public SPAC shareholders receive 1 Pubco Class A share per CEPO share (no cash redemption alternative). The deal includes a $400M cash PIPE, $574.7M convertible notes, $301.9M preferred stock, 5,021 BTC equity PIPE, and a 25,000 BTC contribution from BSTR. Shareholders vote on June 26, 2026; close expected shortly after.

About: Cantor Equity Partners I, Inc. does not have significant operations. The company intends to effect a merger, capital share exchange, asset acquisition, share purchase, reorganization, or related business combination with one or more businesses. It intends to focus its search on targets in financial services, healthcare, real estate services, technology, and software industries. Cantor Equity Partners I, Inc. was incorporated in 2020 and is based in New York, New York.

  • Counterparty: BSTR Holdings, Inc.
  • Offer price: 10.00
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2025-07-16
  • Expected Close: late Q2 2026

item.title: 🎯 Tender · GNK
item.ns0:headline: Rejects Diana's $24.80/share tender; urges WHITE proxy vote
item.description:
Rejects Diana's $24.80/share tender; urges WHITE proxy vote

Genco Shipping & Trading's board recommends shareholders reject Diana Shipping's unsolicited tender offer of $24.80 per share in cash, calling it inadequate as it represents a discount to net asset value and lacks a control premium. The board also urges shareholders to vote the WHITE proxy card in favor of Genco's nominees in the concurrent proxy contest. Diana is seeking to replace the entire Genco board and acquire the company on the cheap.

About: Genco Shipping & Trading Limited, together with its subsidiaries, engages in the ocean transportation of drybulk cargoes worldwide. It operates through two segments: major bulk and minor bulk fleet. The company owns and operates dry bulk vessels to transports iron ore, grains, coal, steel products, and other drybulk cargoes. It charters its vessels primarily to trading houses, including commodities traders; producers; and government-owned entities.

  • Counterparty: Diana Shipping Inc.
  • Offer price: $24.80 per share
  • Consideration: cash

Px $24.69 · Mkt cap $1.05B · EV $1.32B · Fwd P/E 15.0x · Fwd EV/EBITDA 6.9x · Industrials / Marine Transportation

item.ns0:headline: Spins off Honeywell Aerospace June 29; 1 share per 2 held; record June 15
item.description:
Spins off Honeywell Aerospace June 29; 1 share per 2 held; record June 15

Honeywell announced that its board set a record date of June 15, 2026 for the pro-rata spin-off of its Aerospace Technologies business into Honeywell Aerospace Inc. Eligible shareholders will receive one share of Honeywell Aerospace for every two HON shares held, with cash in lieu of fractional shares. The distribution is expected to be effective June 29, 2026, immediately followed by a 1-for-2 reverse stock split.

Total consideration is $25M, payable via $500k initial escrow deposit and deferred installments, plus seller finance, treasury-held IONau gold assets, and I-ON common stock. The company has already sold the AME and Fabrica product lines for $2.0 million upfront (up to $10.5 million deferred) and announced the $42.5 million all-cash sale of MarkForged to Stratasys, reducing annualized cash burn by approximately $25 million. Disclosed that Fifth Third Bank asserted events of default under its credit agreement, including breaches of the minimum cash covenant ($5M), fixed charge coverage ratio, total funded debt ratio, and failure to pay term loan obligations on April 9, 2026. The lender increased interest to the default rate (2% above margin) and reserved rights to accelerate all obligations. The company stated it does not have sufficient cash to satisfy accelerated payment obligations if the lender exercises its acceleration rights.

It also provides financing services for Leksell Gamma Knife units.

item.description:

PT Telekomunikasi Indonesia (Persero) Tbk updated its proposed share buyback plan, seeking shareholder approval for a buyback of up to Rp4 trillion (approximately 10% of issued shares) to strengthen confidence in long-term value. The buyback may be conducted through or outside the stock exchange over up to 12 months from the GMS approval date of June 8, 2026. The company's Mobile segment offers mobile voice, SMS, and mobile broadband services; and digital services, including financial services, video on demand, music, gaming, IoT, big data analytics, and digital advertising services. item.ns0:headline: Acquires 318 MW Colorado gas assets from Blackstone for $1B; $350M equity offering
item.description:
Acquires 318 MW Colorado gas assets from Blackstone for $1B; $350M equity offering

Announced the acquisition of Mountain Peak Power and Canyon Peak Power, two natural gas peaking facilities totaling 318 MW near Denver, Colorado, from Blackstone for a total transaction value of US$1.0 billion, including US$750 million of assumed debt and US$250 million of equity funded via a concurrent $350 million bought deal common share offering. The assets are 100% contracted under tolling agreements with investment-grade customers for 25-30 years and are expected to add approximately US$80 million of annual Adjusted EBITDA, with immediate low-to-mid single digit accretion to Free Cash Flow per share. Closing is expected early in the fourth quarter of 2026, subject to Canyon Peak Power achieving commercial in-service and customary regulatory approvals.

About: TransAlta Corporation owns, operates, and develops a diverse fleet of electrical power generation assets in Canada, the United States, and Australia. It operates through four segments: Hydro, Wind and Solar, Gas, and Energy Transition. owns and operates hydro, wind and solar, natural gas-fired, and coal-fired facilities.

item.ns0:headline: Acquires Pacific Financial for 0.2633 BANR shares (~$17.44/PFLC share at announce)
item.description:
Acquires Pacific Financial for 0.2633 BANR shares (~$17.44/PFLC share at announce)

Corporation filed a registration statement on Form S-4 to register shares of its common stock to be issued in its acquisition of Pacific Financial Corporation. Under the merger agreement dated April 30, 2026, each outstanding share of Pacific Financial common stock will be converted into 0.2633 shares of Banner common stock (cash in lieu of fractional shares). Based on Banner's closing price of $66.25 on April 29, 2026, the implied per-share consideration was $17.44 and the aggregate transaction value was approximately $177 million. Current Pacific Financial shareholders will own approximately 7% of the combined company. The merger requires approval by Pacific Financial shareholders at a special meeting (date to be announced) and receipt of regulatory approvals.

About: Banner Corporation operates as the bank holding company for Banner Bank that provide commercial banking and financial products and services to individuals, businesses, and public sector entities in the United States. It accepts various deposit instruments, including interest-bearing and non-interest-bearing checking accounts, money market deposit accounts, regular savings accounts, and certificates of deposit, as well as treasury management services and retirement savings plans.

  • Counterparty: Pacific Financial Corporation
  • Offer price: 0.2633 BANR shares per PFLC share
  • Deal value: $177 million
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-04-30

item.description:
Faces proxy contest from Radoff/Torok; vote July 28

Is facing a proxy contest from activist stockholders Bradley Radoff and Michael Torok, who have nominated three director candidates for election at the 2026 Annual Meeting on July 28, 2026. Seer's Board unanimously recommends voting FOR its own seven nominees, arguing the activists' proposals undervalue the company and lack relevant expertise. The activists have also made unsolicited acquisition proposals, the latest on May 14, which the Board rejected as significantly undervaluing Seer's cash and growth potential.

It develops Proteograph Product Suite, an integrated solution that comprises consumables, an automation instrumentation, and software that allows researchers to conduct proteomic studies in therapeutic and diagnostic research, and clinical trials.

item.title: 🤝 Merger · VYNE
item.ns0:headline: Reverse merger with Yarrow Bioscience; ~35.84 VYNE shares per Yarrow share; combined co to trade as YARW
item.description:
Reverse merger with Yarrow Bioscience; ~35.84 VYNE shares per Yarrow share; combined co to trade as YARW

And Yarrow Bioscience Inc. entered into a merger agreement on December 17, 2025 (amended Jan 30, 2026) under which a VYNE subsidiary will merge into Yarrow, with Yarrow surviving as a wholly owned subsidiary and VYNE renamed Yarrow Bioscience. Each outstanding Yarrow share will convert into approximately 35.8441 VYNE shares (estimated as of May 22, 2026, subject to adjustment based on VYNE's net cash at closing). VYNE shareholders will own ~3.0% of the combined company on a fully diluted basis; Yarrow pre-closing financing investors will own ~35.5%; and existing Yarrow holders ~61.5%. The combined company is expected to trade on Nasdaq under the symbol YARW. VYNE shareholders will vote on the issuance of shares and related proposals at a special meeting. A pre-closing cash dividend is also planned for VYNE holders.

About: VYNE Therapeutics Inc., a pharmaceutical company, focuses on developing various therapeutics for the treatment of immuno-inflammatory conditions. The company develops FCD105, a topical combination foam that has completed Phase III clinical trials for the treatment of moderate-to-severe acne vulgaris; and FMX114, a combination of tofacitinib, which is in Phase IIa preclinical trial for the treatment of mild-to-moderate atopic dermatitis.

  • Counterparty: Yarrow Bioscience Inc.
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2025-12-17

item.description:
Sells Hartford Funds to Wellington; $300M cash + participation (est. $1.9B NPV)

The Hartford Insurance Group announced the sale of its Hartford Funds business to Wellington Management for $300 million upfront cash plus ongoing quarterly payments representing 95% of after-tax available cash generated by the combined business for up to 7 years, with an estimated net present value of $1.9 billion. Closing is expected in Q1 2027, subject to regulatory and fund approvals. Hartford Funds will be reported as discontinued operations from Q2 2026, and the company will recognize a $250 million deferred tax asset.

item.description:
Faces activist proxy contest; Star Equity Fund nominates Rick Coleman, seeks removal of two directors

Faces a proxy contest from 5.4% shareholder Star Equity Fund, which nominated Rick Coleman for election to the board and recommends removing incumbent directors Peter Tanous and Thomas Vetrano. The activist cites years of declining revenue (TTM $86M, down 48% from FY22 peak), poor governance, and egregious 2023 executive employment agreements. The company had recently announced a strategic review, but Star Equity says further board change is needed to ensure a good outcome.

About: GEE Group, Inc. provides permanent and temporary professional and industrial staffing and placement services in the United States. The company operates through two segments, Industrial Staffing Services and Professional Staffing Services. item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · Lost · CING
item.ns0:headline: FDA rejects CTx-1301 NDA; CMC issues only, resubmission planned
item.description:
FDA rejects CTx-1301 NDA; CMC issues only, resubmission planned

Announced that the FDA has issued a Complete Response Letter (CRL) for its NDA for CTx-1301 (dexmethylphenidate HCl) for ADHD. The CRL identified specific Chemistry, Manufacturing and Controls (CMC) information requests and raised no concerns regarding clinical safety or efficacy. The company has nearly $30 million cash on hand and expects a prompt resubmission to address the CMC issues.

About: Cingulate Inc., a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, focuses on the development of product candidates for the treatment of attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder. The company's lead product candidates are CTx-1301 (dexmethylphenidate), which is in phase 3 clinical trial, and CTx-1302 (dextroamphetamine) for the treatment of attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorders. It also focuses on developing CTx-2103 for the treatment of anxiety disorders.

  • Announced: 2026-06-02

item.description:

Genco Shipping & Trading's board unanimously rejected Diana Shipping Inc.'s revised unsolicited tender offer of $24.80 per share in cash, calling it inadequate and below NAV ($26.66–$27.10 analyst consensus). The board recommends shareholders not tender and instead vote FOR Genco's six director nominees on the WHITE card, and WITHHOLD on Diana's handpicked slate aiming to take control. The board cites Jefferies and Morgan Stanley fairness opinions upholding the offer's inadequacy.

About: Genco Shipping & Trading Limited, together with its subsidiaries, engages in the ocean transportation of dry bulk cargoes worldwide. The company owns and operates dry bulk carrier vessels to transports iron ore, coal, grains, steel products, and other dry-bulk cargoes. It charters its vessels primarily to trading houses, including commodities traders; producers; and government-owned entities.

Under the agreement, $3M of notes will be converted into common stock at the public offering price and $3M will be paid in cash plus accrued interest, contingent on the closing of the company's underwritten public offering (S-1 filed May 27, 2026). After closing, the notes will be extinguished and note holders receive resale registration rights.

About: LiqTech International, Inc., a clean technology company, designs, develops, produces, markets, and sells automated filtering systems, ceramic silicon carbide liquid applications, and diesel particulate air filters in the United States, Canada, Europe, Asia, and South America. It operates in three segments: Water, Ceramics, and Plastics.

Announced it has resubmitted the Biologics License Application for ONS-5010/LYTENAVA (bevacizumab-vikg) to the FDA for treatment of wet AMD. item.description:
Spins off 80.1% of FedEx Freight to shareholders; now public

Corporation completed the spin-off of its freight subsidiary, FedEx Freight Holding Company, by distributing 80.1% of FedEx Freight shares to FedEx stockholders on June 1, 2026. The distribution was made on a pro-rata basis, and FedEx Freight will operate as an independent publicly traded company. The separation was effectuated through a separation and distribution agreement and ancillary transition services, tax, employee, and IP agreements.

About: FedEx Corporation provides transportation, e-commerce, and business services in the United States and internationally. The company's FedEx Express segment offers express transportation, small-package ground delivery, and freight transportation services; time-critical transportation services; and cross-border enablement, technology, and e-commerce transportation solutions. Its FedEx Ground segment provides day-certain delivery services to businesses and residences.

item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · Lost · OCS
item.ns0:headline: Phase 3 DIAMOND trials miss primary endpoint; discontinues DME program
item.description:
Phase 3 DIAMOND trials miss primary endpoint; discontinues DME program

Oculis announced topline results from its DIAMOND Phase 3 trials of OCS-01 in diabetic macular edema. The primary endpoint of mean change in BCVA at week 52 was not met in both trials; retinal thickness showed substantial reduction but did not translate into visual gain. Company does not plan to pursue FDA filing for OCS-01 in DME and will focus resources on Privosegtor and Licaminlimab programs. Cash position remains strong at $278M as of March 31, 2026.

About: Oculis Holding AG, a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, develops novel topical treatments for ophthalmic diseases for both back- and front-of-the-eye. The company's lead candidate is OCS-01, a topical dexamethasone formulation, which is in Phase 3 clinical trials for the treatment of diabetic macular edema; OCS-02, a topical biologic candidate that is in Phase 2b clinical trials for the treatment for keratoconjunctivitis sicca, or dry eye disease; and OCS-05, a novel neuroprotective agent…

  • Announced: 2026-05-29

Is merging with Armada Acquisition Corp. II (SPAC) and acquiring Pathfinder Digital Assets LLC. Under the Business Combination Agreement dated October 19, 2025, SPAC shareholders receive one Pubco Class A share per SPAC share, and Pathfinder unit holders receive one Pubco Class A share per unit. The transaction values the combined entity at $230M assuming a $10.00 per share price. Concurrent PIPE investments total $214.05M cash plus 600,000 XRP from Advance Funding Subscribers, $10.5M cash plus 200,000 XRP from Delayed Funding Subscribers, and additional XRP contributions from Ripple and the Sponsor. The filing is a proxy statement for the extraordinary general meeting of Armada shareholders to approve the deal.

Acquired 100% of Secured Transportation Services LLC for up to $13.0 million in total consideration, consisting of approximately $6.0 million in cash (net $5.2M after adjustments), $1.0 million in restricted stock at closing, and $6.0 million in stock payable over five anniversaries. The acquisition closed on May 22, 2026, and STS became a subsidiary of Nano through its wholly-owned subsidiary Advanced Fuel Transportation Inc. The sellers also entered into registration rights and restrictive covenant agreements.

About: NANO Nuclear Energy Inc. operates as a microreactor technology company. The company is developing ZEUS, a solid-core battery reactor, and ODIN, a low-pressure coolant reactor. The company was founded in 2021 and is based in New York, New York.

item.description:
Receiver files Seventh Interim Report; audit status, misappropriation update

Remains under court-appointed receivership. The receiver's Seventh Interim Report covers activities through April 21, 2026, including the resignation of the independent auditor, status of audits for FY2023 and FY2024, accounting of misappropriated funds, real property and storage matters, and pending litigation. The receivership is ongoing with no resolution yet.

About: Global Tech Industries Group, Inc. operates an online cryptocurrency trading platform in the United States. It operates Beyond Blockchain, a cryptocurrency trading platform, which allows multi-currency clearing and direct settlements in Bitcoin, Ethereum, Tether, Bitcoin Cash, Litecoin, Bitcoin SV, Aave, Compound, Uniswap, Chainlink, and Yearn Finance. The company was formerly known as Tree Top Industries, Inc. and changed its name to Global Tech Industries Group, Inc. in July 2016.

Acquires Archenia for $10M notes + earn-out; vote July 1

Agreed to acquire Archenia, a performance-based marketing technology company, for $10M in convertible promissory notes (convertible at $1.80/share) plus earn-out consideration of up to 2M shares per year for two years based on revenue/EBITDA targets. The sellers include Chairman Russell Horowitz and Vice Chairman Michael Arends, requiring majority-of-minority stockholder approval. Special meeting scheduled for July 1, 2026.

About: Marchex, Inc. operates as an analytics and solutions company that helps businesses connect, drive, measure, and convert callers into customers in the United States and Canada. Its products include Marchex Call Analytics, an analytics platform for enterprises, which depend on inbound phone calls to drive sales, appointments, and reservations; Marchex Call Analytics, Conversation Edition that enable actionable insights for enterprise, mid-sized, and small businesses; Text Analytics and Communicati…

  • Counterparty: Archenia, Inc.
  • Deal value: $10M in convertible notes plus earn-out
  • Stake: 100%
  • Consideration: cash+stock

item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · Won · MDAI
item.ns0:headline: Receives FDA De Novo clearance for DeepView System
item.description:
Receives FDA De Novo clearance for DeepView System

Received FDA De Novo classification for its DeepView System for burn indication, authorizing commercial distribution in the US. The system combines multispectral imaging with AI to assess burn wound healing potential. FDA clearance marks a regulatory milestone for the company.

About: Spectral AI, Inc. operates as an artificial intelligence (AI) company. The company focuses on medical diagnostics for faster and accurate treatment decisions in wound care with applications involving patients with burns and diabetic foot ulcers. Its products include DeepView, a predictive diagnostic device that offers clinicians an objective and immediate assessment of a wound's healing potential prior to treatment or other medical intervention. The company is based in Dallas, Texas.

item.description:
Completes split-off of ESG China; 10.4M shares retired

Completed the split-off of its wholly owned subsidiary ESG China Limited, exchanging 100% of ESG China for the surrender and cancellation of 10,432,800 shares of ESG Inc. common stock. Following cancellation, 15,475,468 shares remain outstanding; the company will continue its North America operations through ESG Provisions, Inc. The transaction was previously announced on April 10, 2026.

About: ESG Inc. develops and operates in plant-based ingredients, and food production and distribution businesses. item.ns0:headline: Self-tender for up to 5.72% of equity at ₹250/share; record date June 5
item.description:
Self-tender for up to 5.72% of equity at ₹250/share; record date June 5

Is buying back up to 60 crore equity shares (5.72% of outstanding) at ₹250 per share, for a total consideration of ₹15,000 crore. Shareholder approval was obtained via postal ballot on May 21, 2026. The record date for eligibility is June 5, 2026, and the tender will be executed through the stock exchange mechanism. Promoter group intends to participate. The buyback price represents a 16.3% premium over the 60-day VWAP. ADR holders (1 ADR = 1 equity share) may cancel ADRs and withdraw underlying shares to participate.

About: Wipro Limited operates as information technology (IT), consulting, and business process services company worldwide. It operates through three segments: IT Services, IT Products, and India State Run Enterprise Services (ISRE). item.description:
Faces ZimCal proxy contest; AGM vote June 9

. faces its third consecutive proxy contest from ZimCal, a debt investor seeking board seats and a premium buyout of its trust preferred securities. The company urges shareholders to vote for its three nominees on the WHITE proxy card, citing record financial performance and a 452% total shareholder return since its transformation. The annual meeting is scheduled for June 9, 2026.

About: Medallion Financial Corp., together with its subsidiaries, operates as a finance company in the United States. The company operates through four segments: Recreation Lending, Home Improvement Lending, Commercial Lending, and Medallion Lending. It provides loans that finance consumer purchases of recreational vehicles, boats, and trailers; consumer home improvements; commercial businesses; and taxi medallions to individuals, and small to mid-size businesses.

The subscription offering is open to eligible depositors and employee benefit plans, with a community offering for unsubscribed shares. Completion requires sale of at least 6,587,500 shares and regulatory and shareholder approvals.

item.ns0:headline: Acquires Skip Dynamix drone assets for up to $7.5M cash/shares/earn-out
item.description:
Acquires Skip Dynamix drone assets for up to $7.5M cash/shares/earn-out

Entered into a definitive asset purchase agreement to acquire substantially all of Skip Dynamix's drone technology business. The purchase price is up to US$7,525,000, comprising $2.525M cash at closing, $2.5M in Draganfly shares via a special warrant, and up to $2.5M in earn-out. The transaction is expected to close in early June 2026, subject to regulatory and exchange approvals.

About: Draganfly Inc. manufactures and sells commercial unmanned aerial vehicles worldwide. Its products include quadcopters, fixed wing aircrafts, ground based robots, and handheld controllers, as well as software used for tracking, live streaming, flight training, and data collection. The company also offers custom engineering and training, simulation consulting, and flight training services, as well as wireless video systems.

item.ns0:headline: FDA approves ANDA for Gallium Ga-68 PSMA-11
item.description:
FDA approves ANDA for Gallium Ga-68 PSMA-11

Ionetix Corporation announced FDA approval of its Abbreviated New Drug Application for Gallium Ga-68 gozetotide (PSMA-11), a radioactive diagnostic agent for PSMA-PET imaging in prostate cancer. The approval makes Ionetix the first commercial drug manufacturer to receive ANDA approval for this drug. The product is supplied in single-dose syringes with a three-hour expiration.

item.title: 🤝 Merger · GLBE
item.ns0:headline: Acquires Passport for $350M (half cash, half stock); close July 2026
item.description:
Acquires Passport for $350M (half cash, half stock); close July 2026

Agreed to acquire Passport Global Inc., a US-based cross-border e-commerce logistics company, for $350 million in upfront consideration funded by approximately equal portions of cash and ordinary shares, plus up to $75 million contingent on Passport achieving certain 2026 financial results. Passport is expected to generate ~$100M revenue in 2026; the transaction is expected to close in early July 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals. Passport's founder and CEO Alex Yancher will join Global-e's executive management team to lead the integrated business.

About: Global-E Online Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, provides a platform to enable and accelerate direct-to-consumer cross-border e-commerce in Israel, the United Kingdom, the United States, and internationally. Its platform enables international shoppers to buy online and merchants to sell from, and to, worldwide. Global-E Online Ltd. was incorporated in 2013 and is headquartered in Petah Tikva, Israel.

  • Counterparty: Passport Global Inc.
  • Deal value: $350M upfront + up to $75M contingent
  • Consideration: cash+stock
  • Announced: 2026-05-26
  • Expected Close: early July 2026
